title: Testing, Static Quality, Performance, and Release · Independent Script Trial
summary: Harden the service-data client with behavioral tests, deterministic fixtures, performance evidence, and a clean release. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a tested and release-ready JavaScript application with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design tests around observable behavior, changed inputs, boundaries, and failures rather than implementation lines.
Use fakes, stubs, spies, and controlled clocks only at owned boundaries without mocking away the behavior under test.
Await asynchronous outcomes, control timers and network fixtures, and fail on unhandled work deterministically.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.javascript:
// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.
// Add one runnable behavior, assertion, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — A release candidate passes the familiar 6-record fixture but fails an empty list and a duplicate identifier. Reproduce both, add behavior and accessibility regressions, and reject release until the clean build agrees.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for design tests around observable behavior, changed inputs, boundaries, and failures rather than implementation lines.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write the lab evidence note for Release Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A release candidate passes the familiar 6-record fixture but fails an empty list and a duplicate identifier. Reproduce both, add behavior and accessibility regressions, and reject release until the clean build agrees. Competency probe: Produce a clean-environment build, supported-runtime record, accessibility checks, rollback plan, and stakeholder acceptance evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Release Evidence through the concrete a tested and release-ready JavaScript application case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Produce a clean-environment build, supported-runtime record, accessibility checks, rollback plan, and stakeholder acceptance evidence.
